title: Run degraded-probe ablation experiment
description: Degrade first-letter probes to F1=0.84 (matching city-continent RAVEL quality) and F1=0.79 (matching city-country). Remeasure absorption rates at L24. Compare degraded-first-letter absorption rates to RAVEL absorption rates. This experiment definitively resolves whether cross-domain variation is genuine or a probe artifact.
impact: Resolves the primary confound. If degraded rates match RAVEL rates, reframe paper as 'layer-dependence + causal evidence' and acknowledge cross-domain variation is probe-driven. If degraded rates differ from RAVEL rates, the cross-domain finding gains substantial credibility.

title: Recalibrate primary claim strength based on AI-01 results
description: If the degraded-probe ablation confirms the confound: (1) Elevate layer-dependence to the paper's primary contribution (unconfounded, 15x variation, F1=0.97 at all layers). (2) Present cross-domain variation as 'suggestive, confounded by probe quality.' (3) Update abstract, Introduction contribution list, and Conclusion accordingly. If the ablation rules out the confound: keep current framing but cite the ablation as direct evidence.
impact: Aligns claims with evidence. Prevents reviewer rejection on overclaiming grounds.

title: Promote restricted activation patching analysis to main text
description: The restricted analysis (raw accuracy >= 0.50, N=12 words, 38.2% vs 1.1%, p<0.001) is buried in the consolidation data. Move it to Section 5.1 as a primary result alongside the full-sample result. Report both analyses in Table 5 with a clear column indicating sample restriction. The restricted analysis is STRONGER (larger delta, high-confidence tokens only) and addresses the selection-bias concern.
impact: Strengthens the causal evidence claim by showing the effect survives on representative tokens.

title: Fix all broken cross-references
description: (1) Replace 'Table 7 in Section 4.4 of the extended results' in Section 8.2 with inline numbers from consolidation data (L24-16k: 50.0% absorbed, L6-16k: 100.0% absorbed) or remove the claim entirely. (2) Change 'Section 8.6' in Section 3.5 to 'Section 8.5'. (3) Either write appendix sections B-D or remove all 'in Appendix' references from main text.
impact: Eliminates manuscript-preparation flags that erode reviewer trust.

title: Generate missing figures 5 and 6
description: Figure 5: Paired dot plot of activation patching results. Each dot-pair is one word; left dot = control recovery rate, right dot = child-zeroed recovery rate. Lines connecting pairs show the treatment effect. Color by raw accuracy (threshold at 0.50 for restricted subset). Generated from phase0/activation_patching_full.json. Figure 6: Stacked bar chart for hedging decomposition. Three stacked segments (7.9% strict / 86.2% compensatory / 5.9% persistent) for first-letter multi-L0. Include city-language bar with N=3 caveat annotation. Generated from hedging data.
impact: Provides visual support for Sections 5.1 and 5.2, the paper's second-strongest results.

title: Change the paper title
description: The current title 'The Absorption Tax: ...' leads with a failed theoretical framework. Replace with a title reflecting the actual contributions. Candidates: (a) 'Beyond First-Letter Spelling: Cross-Layer and Cross-Domain Characterization of SAE Feature Absorption', (b) 'Feature Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders Varies 15-Fold Across Model Layers: A Multi-Domain Analysis', (c) 'Layer-Dependent Feature Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders: From Spelling to Entity-Attribute Hierarchies'.
impact: Aligns title with content. Prevents immediate reviewer skepticism when they discover the 'Absorption Tax' failed.

title: Resolve probe F1 discrepancy across sections
description: Audit which probe was used for each experiment: (a) first-letter absorption measurement at L6/L12/L18/L24, (b) activation patching at L12, (c) cross-domain absorption at L24. The consolidation records probe_f1=0.883 for activation patching; the paper implies F1>=0.97. Section 3.3 says sklearn probes were used; the data shows ICL probes at position -6 with F1=1.0. Reconcile and state the actual probe identity and F1 for each experiment explicitly.
impact: Eliminates a factual inconsistency that reviewers will catch.

title: Remove or heavily qualify the claim that hierarchy matters more than architecture
description: The comparison of hierarchy p=0.005 (from L24 cross-domain) vs. architecture p=0.87 (from L12 comparison) is invalid: different layers, different data, different confounds, non-nested tests. Remove 'hierarchy type explains more variance than architecture choice' from Discussion and Conclusion. Replace with: 'We find no significant architecture effect at layer 12 (p=0.87, N=16, underpowered); whether architecture matters at L24 -- where absorption is most severe -- could not be tested due to limited architecture availability.'
impact: Removes an unsupported causal claim that a statistically literate reviewer will flag.

title: Render Figure 2 (pipeline schematic)
description: Figure 2 exists only as fig2_pipeline_desc.md. A method paper at NeurIPS/ICLR needs a proper pipeline diagram. Convert the markdown description into a TikZ diagram or programmatic figure showing: residual stream extraction -> probe classification -> FN detection -> IG attribution -> absorption detection -> activation patching (causal validation branch).
impact: Completes visual communication requirements for the Method section.

title: Discuss L12-to-L24 reversal as probe quality evidence
description: The pilot (L12) showed semantic > first-letter absorption. The full results (L24) show first-letter > semantic absorption. This reversal coincides with probe quality improvement at L24. Add a paragraph in the Discussion explicitly noting that the direction of the cross-domain comparison REVERSED when better probes became available, and interpret this as further evidence that probe quality drives measured absorption rates.
impact: Demonstrates intellectual honesty and strengthens the probe-quality confound analysis.

title: Trim abstract to under 200 words
description: Current abstract is ~250 words. Lead with layer-dependence (unconfounded) and causal evidence (d=1.33). Cover cross-domain variation, hedging, and negative detectors in one sentence each. Remove specific rho values for GAS/CMI/Tax from abstract.
impact: Improves readability; meets venue length requirements.

title: Verify timing data and full-mode execution fidelity
description: The consolidation reports 5 minutes total wall clock for experiments planned at 10+ hours. Phase0 activation patching took 2 minutes (planned 60 min). Verify that activation_patching_full.json contains genuine per-word per-context results for 200 contexts x 25 words, not cached pilot data. If cached, relabel as 'PILOT+' rather than 'FULL MODE'.
impact: Ensures the 'full mode' label is accurate.

title: Remove cross-domain hedging comparison or add adequate caveats
description: The city-language hedging comparison (N=3 FNs, 66.7% strict) vs. first-letter (N=304, 7.9%) is in the consolidation's 'key findings'. Remove this comparison from the main text or add a prominent caveat: 'This comparison is based on N=3 false negatives for city-language and should not be interpreted quantitatively.'

title: Write appendix sections or remove appendix references
description: The paper references Appendix B (GAS), C (CMI), D (Absorption Tax), and 'full results in Appendix' (Table 5). None of these appendix sections exist. Either write them from existing JSON data (gas_full.json, cmi_l0_22.json, tax_qualitative.json, activation_patching_full.json) or remove all appendix references.

title: Document probe identity and probe position for each experiment
description: Create a supplementary table listing: experiment name, probe type (sklearn vs. ICL), token position (-1 vs. -2 vs. -6), probe F1, layer, hierarchy. This resolves the three-way mismatch identified by the section critique and supports reproducibility.
impact: Supports reproducibility and resolves internal inconsistency.
